| FIFA min           = 190
| FIFA min date      = July–August 2016<ref name=FIFA/>
| First game         = {{plainlist|
*''Unofficial''<br />{{fb|ALB|1992}} [[1993 Albania v Kosovo football match|3–1]] {{fb-rt|Kosova|name=Kosovo}}<br />([[Tirana]], Albania; 14 February 1993)
*''Official''<br />{{fb|KOS}} [[2014 Kosovo v Haiti football match|0–0]] {{fb-rt|HAI}}<br />([[Mitrovica, Kosovo|Mitrovica]], Kosovo; 5 March 2014)}}
| Largest win        = {{plainlist|
*''Unofficial''<br />{{fb|MON}} 1–7 {{fb-rt|KOS|UNMIK}}<br />([[Cap d'Ail]], France; 22 April 2006)
*''Official''<br />{{fb|MLT}} 0–5 {{fb-rt|KOS}}<br />([[Ta' Qali]], Malta; 17 November 2018)<br />{{fb|KOS}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|BFA}}<br />([[Pristina]], Kosovo; 24 March 2022)}}
| Largest loss       = {{fb|KOS}} 0–6 {{fb-rt|CRO}}<br />([[Shkodër]], Albania; 6 October 2016)

The '''Kosovo national football team''' ({{langx|sq|Kombëtarja e futbollit të Kosovës}}; {{langx|sr|Фудбалска репрезентација Косова|Fudbalska reprezentacija Kosova}}) represents [[Kosovo]] in men's international [[association football|football]]. The team is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Kosovo]], the governing body for [[football in Kosovo]], and is under the jurisdiction of [[FIFA]] globally.

==History==
===Pre-independence===
====First match====
On 29 November 1942, Kosovo for first time in its history played a [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly match]] as part of the celebrations for 30th Anniversary of the Independence of Albania against [[Albania national football team|Tirana]]{{efn|name=Tirana|The alternative name of the [[Albania national football team|Albania national team]] that was used during this match.<ref name=Shehu/>}} and the match ended with a 2–0 away defeat. The starting line-up of that match was Mustafa Daci ([[Goalkeeper (association football)|GK]]), Ballanca, Ahmet Zaimi, Mazllum Xërxa, Veseli, Hajdar Hamza, Nebil Dylatahu, Ramadan Vraniqi, Dobrica Barbaroga,{{efn|Dobrica Barbaroga was the Albanianized full name of Dobrica Barbarogić by the media of that time, which may have been expression of peaceful coexistence with national minorities in Kosovo at that time.<ref name=DizdariI/>}} Bajrami and Henci.<ref name=DizdariI>{{cite web|url=http://www.zemrashqiptare.net/news/1668/rp-0/act-print/rf-1/printo.html|title=Besnik Dizdari: Kur në Tiranë lindte Përfaqësuesja e Kosovës...(II)|trans-title=Besnik Dizdari: When in Tirana the Kosovo national team was born...(II)|language=sq|author=Besnik Dizdari|website=Zemra Shqiptare|date=12 December 2007|access-date=14 May 2020|archive-date=9 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220109044136/http://www.zemrashqiptare.net/news/1668/rp-0/act-print/rf-1/printo.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name=DizdariII>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/opinion-nga-besnik-dizdari-armiqte-e-vertete-dhe-miqte-e-rreme-te-kosoves/|title="Armiqtë" e vërtetë dhe "miqtë" e rremë të Kosovës|trans-title=The real "enemies" and false "friends" of Kosovo|language=sq|author=Besnik Dizdari|publisher=[[Panorama Sport]]|date=15 September 2019|access-date=19 April 2020|archive-date=3 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803042502/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/opinion-nga-besnik-dizdari-armiqte-e-vertete-dhe-miqte-e-rreme-te-kosoves/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name=Shehu>{{cite news|url=https://gazetasi.al/kalvari-sportiv-i-kosoves-dhe-miqesorja-e-harruar-me-jugosllavine/|title=Kosova, kalvari sportiv e miqësorja e harruar me Jugosllavinë|trans-title=Kosovo, the sports ordeal and the forgotten friendly with Yugoslavia|language=sq|author=Mendrit Shehu|website=Gazetasi|date=18 November 2019|access-date=19 April 2020|archive-date=3 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803005151/https://gazetasi.al/kalvari-sportiv-i-kosoves-dhe-miqesorja-e-harruar-me-jugosllavine/|url-status=live}}</ref>

====Yugoslav era====
{{quote box|width=36%|align=right|quote="The purpose of this [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly match]] is that we wanted to face a team that has the same temperament and character as [[Albania national football team|Albania national team]]."|source=—The reasoning of the then head coach of [[Yugoslavia national football team|Yugoslavia]], [[Rajko Mitić]] for the reason for organizing the Kosovo–Yugoslavia match.<ref name=Shehu/>}}
On 8 November 1967, Kosovo for first time as [[Socialist Autonomous Province of Kosovo#1963–1968|autonomous province]] of [[Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia|SFR Yugoslavia]] played a friendly match against Yugoslavia and the match ended with a 3–3 home draw. The starting line-up of that match was a mix between Albanian and Serbian players as Milosavlević, Stevanović, Mušikić, Abrashi, S. Džukić, V. Džukić, Brovina, Hatibi, Radović, [[Luan Prekazi|Prekazi]] and Pindović. For Yugoslavia, this match it was preparation before the [[UEFA Euro 1968 qualifying Group 4|UEFA Euro 1968 qualifying]] match against [[Albania national football team|Albania]].

Yugoslav squads at international competitions often included players from Kosovo, such as [[Fadil Vokrri]] and [[Stevan Stojanović]]. Three other footballers from Kosovo, [[Fahrudin Jusufi]], [[Milutin Šoškić]] and [[Vladimir Durković]], were part of the Yugoslav team that won the gold medal at the [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960 Summer Olympics]] and silver medal at the [[1960 European Nations' Cup]].

====After breakup of Yugoslavia====
On 20 January 1993, The [[Football Federation of Kosovo]] signed a cooperation protocol with the [[Albanian Football Association]] and in the framework of this protocol it was decided to play on 14 February a [[1993 Albania v Kosovo football match|friendly match]] between [[Albania national football team|Albania]] and Kosovo, which was the first match of Kosovo following the [[breakup of Yugoslavia]].<ref name=Mevetesimi>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/FFK_20-vjet-Mevetesimi.pdf|title=Futbolli 1991–2011, 20 vjet mëvetësim|trans-title=Football 1991–2019, 20 years independence|pages=Respectively on pages 64 and 65, contain information about cooperation protocol and the match|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=2011|access-date=23 April 2020|archive-date=2 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200802225434/http://ffk-kosova.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/FFK_20-vjet-Mevetesimi.pdf|url-status=live}}</ref> This match ended with a 1–3 defeat. The starting line-up of that match was Ahmet Beselica (GK), [[Ardian Kozniku]], Bardhec Seferi, Fadil Berisha, Gani Llapashtica, [[Genc Hoxha]], [[Isa Sadriu]], [[Kushtrim Munishi]], [[Muharrem Sahiti]], Sadullah Ajeti and Selajdin Jerliu.<ref name=RSSSFreport>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1993e1.html|title=International Matches 1993 – Europe, January–March|quote=14/02/1993, Tiranë, Dinamo, 14000, Friendly International, ALBANIA–KOSOVA|publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation|RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=4 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221204190528/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1993e1.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

====During UN administration====
{{quote box|width=30%|align=right|quote="I am very pleased with the team's performance even though we had predicted a stronger opponent. However, I want to emphasize that our players have been fantastic. The fact that we do not play many international matches may have inspired the players to give it their all this time."|source=—Opinion of the then coach [[Muharrem Sahiti]] after the match against Monaco.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.albaniasoccer.com/en/index.php?id=337|title=Friendly match/Kosovo defeated Monaco by a score of 7-1|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060522012637/http://www.albaniasoccer.com/en/index.php?id=337|website=AlbaniaSoccer|date=22 April 2006|archive-date=22 May 2006}}</ref>}}
On 7 September 2002, Kosovo for first time after the [[Kosovo War]] played a [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly match]] against Albania and the match ended with a 0–1 home defeat. The starting line-up of that match was Ahmet Beselica (GK), [[Ardian Kozniku]], [[Arsim Abazi]], Besnik Kollari, Fadil Ademi, Faruk Statovci, [[Ismet Munishi]], [[Mehmet Dragusha]], Sunaj Keqi, [[Xhevdet Llumnica]] and [[Zenun Selimi]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/2002e3.html|title=Kosova 0:1 Albania|publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation|RSSSF]]|date=7 September 2002|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=27 November 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221127190926/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/2002e3.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 15 June 2007, Kosovo won 1–0 over [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]]. It was the first time that Kosovo played against a team that has taken part in the [[FIFA World Cup]]. The winning goal was scored by [[Kristian Nushi]] from the [[Penalty kick (association football)|penalty kick]] on the 84th minute.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.futbolli.com/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=3359|title=Fitore historike kunder Arabise - Nushi "sheiku" i Kosovës|trans-title=Historic win against Arabia - Nushi "sheik" of Kosovo|quote=Emri i Kristian Nushit pa dyshim do të hyjë në historinë e futbollit kosovarë. Në realitet, futbollisti i deritashëm i skuadrës zvicerane, Vill, ka shënuar golin e fitores në minutën e 84-të në ndeshjen miqësore ndërkombëtare ndaj Arabisë Saudite qe perfundoi me rezultatin e ngushte 1:0. Nushi u tregua i saktë nga pika e bardhë, pasi më parë mbrojtësit sauditë rrëzuan brenda zonës Ismet Ramushin dhe gjyqtari pa u hamendur tregoi në pikën e bardhë|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071130071849/http://www.futbolli.com/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=3359|language=sq|website=Futbolli.com|date=7 September 2002|archive-date=30 November 2007}}</ref> On 22 April 2006, Kosovo achieved their biggest win, defeating [[Monaco national football team|Monaco]] 7–1. If one included matches played before Kosovo became a FIFA member, this scoreline is their best result yet.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.forumishqiptar.com/threads/66156-P%C3%ABrfaq%C3%ABsuesja-e-Kosov%C3%ABs-n%C3%AB-futboll|title=Përfaqësuesja e Kosovës në futboll|trans-title=Kosovo national team in football|quote=Përfaqësuesja e Kosovës mposhti atë të Monacos me rezultat 7:1|language=sq|website=Forumi Shqiptar|date=22 April 2006|access-date=16 August 2018|archive-date=25 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180925025759/http://www.forumishqiptar.com/threads/66156-P%C3%ABrfaq%C3%ABsuesja-e-Kosov%C3%ABs-n%C3%AB-futboll|url-status=live}}</ref>

===After declaration of independence===
On 6 May 2008, after its [[2008 Kosovo declaration of independence|declaration of independence]] from [[Serbia]], Kosovo applied for [[FIFA]] membership. On 24 October 2008, Kosovo's application was discussed at the [[FIFA Congress]] in [[Zürich]] and Kosovo's bid was rejected. At the occasion, FIFA determined Kosovo could not play friendly matches against FIFA members' national teams.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/federation/bodies/media/newsid=924258.html|title=FIFA's finances solid|quote=An application for affiliation from the Kosovo football association was deemed not to comply with art. 10 of the FIFA Statutes, which stipulates that only "an independent state recognised by the international community" may be admitted. In accordance with the Statutes, Kosovo cannot play any friendly matches|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081029133912/http://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/federation/bodies/media/newsid=924258.html|publisher=[[FIFA]]|date=24 October 2008|archive-date=29 October 2008|access-date=4 December 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 17 February 2010, for the first time since its declaration of independence, Kosovo played an unofficial friendly match against [[Albania national football team|Albania]], which ended with a 2–3 home defeat for Kosovo. The starting line-up of that match was [[Kushtrim Mushica]] (GK), [[Anel Rashkaj]], Dukagjin Gashi, Enis Zabërgja, [[Fisnik Papuçi]], [[Ilir Nallbani]], Liridon Kukaj, Robert Gjeraj, [[Shpëtim Hasani]] and [[Yll Hoxha]]. This match had a charity character as the profits gathered from this meeting went to those affected by floods in [[Shkodër]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.albaniasoccer.com/lajme/kosova/perfaqesuesja/1326-miqesore-kosove-shqiperi-2-3-.html|title=Miqesore/Kosove - Shqipëri 2-3|trans-title=Friendly/Kosovo - Albania 2-3|language=sq|website=AlbaniaSoccer|date=17 February 2010|access-date=13 June 2018|archive-date=7 June 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190607003142/http://www.albaniasoccer.com/lajme/kosova/perfaqesuesja/1326-miqesore-kosove-shqiperi-2-3-.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

In September 2012, [[List of Albania international footballers|Albania international]] [[Lorik Cana]], along with [[List of Switzerland international footballers|Swiss internationals]] [[Granit Xhaka]], [[Valon Behrami]] and [[Xherdan Shaqiri]], all of them with [[Kosovo Albanians|Kosovo Albanian]] origin, wrote a declaration to FIFA President [[Sepp Blatter]], asking him to allow Kosovo to play friendly matches. The declaration was also signed by eight other Albanian footballers hailing from Kosovo as [[Ahmed Januzi]], [[Alban Meha]], [[Armend Dallku]], [[Burim Kukeli]], [[Etrit Berisha]], [[Fatmire Alushi|Fatmire Bajramaj]], [[Mërgim Mavraj]] and [[Samir Ujkani]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://gazeta-shqip.com/lajme/2012/09/20/shaqiri-xhaka-canaco-deklarate-per-kosoven-ne-fifa/|title=Shaqiri, Xhaka, Cana&Co., deklaratë për Kosovën në FIFA|trans-title=Shaqiri, Xhaka, Cana&Co., statement for Kosovo in FIFA|language=sq|website=GazetaShqip|date=20 September 2012|access-date=11 March 2014|archive-date=1 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160601202326/http://www.gazeta-shqip.com/lajme/2012/09/20/shaqiri-xhaka-canaco-deklarate-per-kosoven-ne-fifa/|url-status=live}}</ref> Some players, especially [[Kosovo Serbs]] as [[Milan Biševac]] and [[Miloš Krasić]], continued to play for [[Serbia national football team|Serbia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zurnal.rs/2012/09/21/cana-saciri-dzaka-i-behrami-pisali-blateru-zbog-kosova/index.html|title=Cana, Šaćiri, Džaka i Behrami pisali Blateru zbog Kosova|trans-title=Cana, Shaqiri, Xhaka dhe Behrami wrote to Blater about Kosovo|language=sr|publisher=[[Sportski žurnal]]|date=21 September 2012|access-date=24 September 2012|archive-date=7 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160307211231/http://zurnal.rs/2012/09/21/cana-saciri-dzaka-i-behrami-pisali-blateru-zbog-kosova/index.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zurnal.rs/2012/10/02/vokri-fifa-i-uefa-imaju-dvostruke-standarde/index.html|title=Vokri: FIFA i UEFA imaju dvostruke standarde|trans-title=Vokrri: FIFA and UEFA have double standards|language=sr|publisher=[[Sportski žurnal]]|date=4 October 2012|access-date=4 October 2012|archive-date=7 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160307225050/http://zurnal.rs/2012/10/02/vokri-fifa-i-uefa-imaju-dvostruke-standarde/index.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

====Permission by FIFA to play friendlies====
{{football squad on pitch|align=right
|GK_nat  = |GK  = [[Samir Ujkani|'''Ujkani''']]
|RB_nat  = |RB  = [[Loret Sadiku|'''Sadiku''']]
|RCB_nat = |RCB = [[Avni Pepa|'''Pepa''']]
|LCB_nat = |LCB = [[Fanol Përdedaj|'''Përdedaj''']]
|LB_nat  = |LB  = [[Kristian Nushi|'''Nushi''']]
|RM_nat  = |RM  = [[Ardian Gashi|'''Gashi''']]
|CM_nat  = |CM  = [[Anel Rashkaj|'''Rashkaj''']] '''([[Captain (association football)|c]])'''
|LM_nat  = |LM  = [[Enis Alushi|'''Alushi''']]
|RF_nat  = |RF  = [[Shpëtim Hasani|'''Hasani''']]
|CF_nat  = |CF  = [[Albert Bunjaku|'''Bunjaku''']]
|LF_nat  = |LF  = [[Ilir Azemi|'''Azemi''']]
|caption = Kosovo starting line-up in first international friendly against Haiti<br />'''(4–3–3)'''<ref>{{cite news|url=https://topsporti.com/artikulli/zyrtarizohet-11-shja-kosovare/ffk-superliga-e-kosoves|title=Zyrtarizohet 11-shja kosovare|trans-title=The Kosovan XI is officialized|language=sq|website=TopSporti|date=5 March 2014|access-date=5 March 2019|archive-date=6 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190306043756/https://topsporti.com/artikulli/zyrtarizohet-11-shja-kosovare/ffk-superliga-e-kosoves|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://topsporti.com/artikulli/fitore-e-kosoves/ffk-superliga-e-kosoves|title=Kosova - Haiti, luhet historia|trans-title=Kosovo - Haiti, the history is played|language=sq|website=TopSporti|date=5 March 2014|access-date=5 March 2019|archive-date=6 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190306044102/https://topsporti.com/artikulli/fitore-e-kosoves/ffk-superliga-e-kosoves|url-status=live}}</ref>
}}
On 6 February 2013, FIFA decided to allow Kosovan club teams to play friendly games against clubs from countries whose national teams were members of FIFA.<ref name=":0">{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/administration/02/01/19/65/circularno.1342-friendlygameswithteamsofthefootballfederationofkosovo.pdf|title=FIFA Circular no.1342|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190620192102/https://www.fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/administration/02/01/19/65/circularno.1342-friendlygameswithteamsofthefootballfederationofkosovo.pdf|publisher=[[FIFA]]|date=6 February 2013|archive-date=20 June 2019}}</ref> However, it was stipulated that Kosovan clubs and teams could not display national symbols such as the Kosovan flag, emblem, etc., or play the Kosovan anthem.<ref name=":0" />

On 5 March 2014, the Kosovan football team was allowed by FIFA to play its [[2014 Kosovo v Haiti football match|first international friendly]] match, against [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]]; the match ended in a 0–0 draw.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/2014/03/05/kosova-e-dha-provimin-barazoi-pa-gola-me-haitin/|title=Kosova e dha provimin, barazoi pa gola me Haitin|trans-title=Kosovo passed the exam, draw without goals with Haiti|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=5 March 2014|access-date=5 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190306042847/http://ffk-kosova.com/2014/03/05/kosova-e-dha-provimin-barazoi-pa-gola-me-haitin/|archive-date=6 March 2019|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-26451936|title=Kosovo footballers draw with Haiti in Mitrovica debut|publisher=[[BBC News]]|date=5 March 2014|access-date=21 June 2018|archive-date=12 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191212032555/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-26451936|url-status=live}}</ref> Before the match, some players of Kosovo posed with [[gun]]s at a [[shooting range]], causing a stir in [[Serbia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/news-and-comment/kosovo-national-team-cause-a-stir-by-posing-with-guns-before-first-ever-international-fixture-9177063.html|title=Kosovo national team cause a stir by posing with guns before first ever international fixture|work=[[The Independent]]|date=7 March 2014|access-date=4 December 2017|archive-date=14 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171014034727/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/news-and-comment/kosovo-national-team-cause-a-stir-by-posing-with-guns-before-first-ever-international-fixture-9177063.html|url-status=live}}</ref> On the eve of the match, supporters of Kosovo burned the [[flag of Serbia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.b92.net/sport/fudbal/vesti.php?yyyy=2014&mm=03&dd=05&nav_id=819908|title=KM: Zapaljena zastava Srbije|trans-title=KM: Burned flag of Serbia|language=sr|publisher=[[B92]]|date=5 March 2014|access-date=13 June 2018|archive-date=13 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180613235118/https://www.b92.net/sport/fudbal/vesti.php?yyyy=2014&mm=03&dd=05&nav_id=819908|url-status=live}}</ref> This incident prompted the Football Association of Serbia to request that FIFA revoked Kosovo's right to play international friendlies.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.b92.net/sport/fudbal/vesti.php?yyyy=2014&mm=03&dd=06&nav_id=820324|title=FSS traži opoziv FIFA za Kosovo|trans-title=FAS seeking revocation of FIFA for Kosovo|language=sr|publisher=[[B92]]|date=6 March 2014|access-date=13 June 2018|archive-date=13 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180613235158/https://www.b92.net/sport/fudbal/vesti.php?yyyy=2014&mm=03&dd=06&nav_id=820324|url-status=live}}</ref>

After the match against Haiti, six more matches were held in 2014, against [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]], [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]], [[Oman national football team|Oman]], [[Equatorial Guinea national football team|Equatorial Guinea]] and [[Albania national football team|Albania]]. The first win in these friendlies was against Oman, by 1–0,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2014/09/07/kosova-shijoi-fitoren-e-pare-nen-ombrellen-e-fifa-s/|title=Kosova shijoi fitoren e parë nën ombrellën e FIFA-s|trans-title=Kosovo relish the first victory under FIFA umbrella|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=7 September 2014|access-date=10 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180810110246/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2014/09/07/kosova-shijoi-fitoren-e-pare-nen-ombrellen-e-fifa-s/|archive-date=10 August 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> while the biggest defeat a 1–6 against Turkey.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/?p=7043|title=Kosova pësoi humbje nga Turqia|trans-title=Kosovo suffered losses from Turkey|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=21 May 2014|access-date=10 August 2018|archive-date=12 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170912030214/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/?p=7043|url-status=live}}</ref>

====Membership in UEFA and FIFA====
{{See also|Membership of Kosovo in international sports federations}}
In September 2015, at an [[UEFA]] Executive Committee meeting in [[Malta]], the request from Kosovo for admission in UEFA was scheduled for deliberation in the next Ordinary Congress, to be held in [[Budapest]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.zeriamerikes.com/a/kosovo-uefa/2969817.html|title=UEFA shqyrton në mars të vitit të ardhshëm anëtarësimin e Kosovës|trans-title=UEFA reviews in March next year Kosovo's membership|language=sq|publisher=[[Voice of America]]|date=19 September 2015|access-date=16 August 2018|archive-date=16 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180816194806/https://www.zeriamerikes.com/a/kosovo-uefa/2969817.html|url-status=live}}</ref> On 3 May 2016, at the Ordinary Congress, Kosovo was accepted into UEFA after members voted 28–24 in favor of Kosovo.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/insideuefa/mediaservices/newsid=2359883.html?redirectFromOrg=true#/football+federation+kosovo+joins+uefa|title=Football Federation of Kosovo joins UEFA|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=3 May 2016|access-date=12 July 2018|archive-date=12 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200412022658/https://www.uefa.com/insideuefa/mediaservices/newsid=2359883.html?redirectFromOrg=true#/football+federation+kosovo+joins+uefa|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2016/may/03/kosovo-accepted-uefa-member-vote-congress|title=Kosovo accepted as member of Uefa after Congress vote|work=[[The Guardian]]|date=3 May 2016|access-date=12 December 2016|archive-date=4 June 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190604132531/https://www.theguardian.com/football/2016/may/03/kosovo-accepted-uefa-member-vote-congress|url-status=live}}</ref> Ten days later, Kosovo was accepted in [[FIFA]] during their 66th Congress in [[Mexico]], with 141 votes in favour and 23 against.<ref>{{Cite news|title=FIFA welcomes Kosovo, Gibraltar as newest members|url=https://www.efe.com/efe/english/sports/fifa-welcomes-kosovo-gibraltar-as-newest-members/50000266-2925607|access-date=2021-07-18|website=www.efe.com|language=en|archive-date=28 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128224606/https://www.efe.com/efe/english/sports/fifa-welcomes-kosovo-gibraltar-as-newest-members/50000266-2925607|url-status=live}}</ref>

Kosovo began to make their debut in the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]], where Kosovo played in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group I|Group I]] with [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]], [[Finland national football team|Finland]], [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]], [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] and [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]].<ref name=2018FIFAWCQ-FIFA>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/kosovo-and-gibraltar-assigned-to-2018-fifa-world-cup-qualifying-groups-2800710|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180614021249/https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/kosovo-and-gibraltar-assigned-to-2018-fifa-world-cup-qualifying-groups-2800710|url-status=dead|archive-date=14 June 2018|title=Kosovo and Gibraltar assigned to 2018 FIFA World Cup qualifying groups|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=9 June 2016}}</ref><ref name=2018FIFAWCQ-FFK>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2016/06/09/kosova-ne-grupin-i-per-kualifikimet-e-boterorit-2018/|title=Kosova në Grupin I për kualifikimet e Botërorit 2018|trans-title=Kosovo in Group I for 2018 World Cup qualifiers|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=9 June 2016|access-date=13 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180614021246/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2016/06/09/kosova-ne-grupin-i-per-kualifikimet-e-boterorit-2018/|archive-date=14 June 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> In their first game, the Kosovars held host Finland to a 1–1 draw,<ref name=FINvKVX>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/kosove-briliante-debuton-me-barazim-ne-kualifikimet-e-boterorit-2018/|title=Kosovë brilante, debuton me barazim në kualifikimet e Botërorit 2018|trans-title=Brilliant Kosovo, debuts with a draw in the 2018 World Cup qualifiers|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=5 September 2016|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=29 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200929040248/https://ffk-kosova.com/kosove-briliante-debuton-me-barazim-ne-kualifikimet-e-boterorit-2018/|url-status=live}}</ref> but lost the remaining games and finished last in the group.

{{football squad on pitch|align=right
|GK_nat  = |GK  = [[Arijanet Muric|'''Muric''']]
|RB_nat  = |RB  = [[Mërgim Vojvoda|'''Vojvoda''']]
|RCB_nat = |RCB = [[Amir Rrahmani|'''Rrahmani''']] '''([[Captain (association football)|c]])'''
|LCB_nat = |LCB = [[Fidan Aliti|'''Aliti''']]
|LB_nat  = |LB  = [[Benjamin Kololli|'''Kololli''']]
|RCM_nat = |RCM = [[Ibrahim Drešević|'''Drešević''']]
|AM_nat  = |AM  = [[Bersant Celina|'''Celina''']]
|LCM_nat = |LCM = [[Valon Berisha|'''Berisha''']]
|RW_nat  = |RW  = [[Milot Rashica|'''Rashica''']]
|CF_nat  = |CF  = [[Vedat Muriqi|'''Muriqi''']]
|LW_nat  = |LW  = [[Arbër Zeneli|'''Zeneli''']]
|caption = Kosovo's most used starting line-up during their fifteen-match unbeaten run '''(4–3–3)'''<ref>{{cite tweet|user=DardanianFooty|number=1603405276421521410|date=15 December 2022|title=With a total of 700 days unbeaten and a streak of 15 matches without a single defeat, 2018/19 Kosovo deserved its nickname as the "Brazil of the Balkans"|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230105050002/https://twitter.com/DardanianFooty/status/1603405276421521410|archive-date=5 January 2023}}</ref><ref name=FIFAplus>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/articles/brazil-of-the-balkans-when-fast-rising-kosovo-surpassed-all-expectations|title=Brazil of the Balkans: When fast-rising Kosovo surpassed all expectations|publisher=[[FIFA]]|date=5 April 2023}}</ref>}}
Following the failure to qualify for [[2018 FIFA World Cup]], Kosovo participated in the [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D]] sharing group with [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]], [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]] and [[Malta national football team|Malta]],<ref name=201819UEFANL-UEFA>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/news/0253-0d81efc3a33f-dd2083a5720d-1000--uefa-nations-league-2018-19-league-phase-draw/|title=UEFA Nations League 2018/19 League Phase draw|quote=Group D3: Azerbaijan, Faroe Islands, Malta, Kosovo|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=24 January 2018|access-date=8 September 2018|archive-date=10 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180910040743/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/news/newsid=2530180.html?iv=true|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name=201819UEFANL-TCH>{{cite news|url=http://top-channel.tv/2018/01/24/liga-e-kombeve-kosova-ne-grupin-3/|title=Liga e Kombeve, Kosova në Grupin D3|trans-title=Nations League, Kosovo in Group D3|language=sq|publisher=[[Top Channel]]|date=24 January 2018|access-date=8 September 2018|archive-date=8 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180908164457/http://top-channel.tv/2018/01/24/liga-e-kombeve-kosova-ne-grupin-3/|url-status=live}}</ref> where the Kosovars topped their group undefeated.<ref name=201819UEFANL-FFKfinal>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/kosova-ben-histori-kalon-ne-play-off-per-euro-2020-dhe-promovohet-ne-ligen-c/|title=Kosova bën histori, kalon në "play-off" për Euro 2020 dhe promovohet në Ligën C|trans-title=Kosovo makes history, goes to the "play-off" for Euro 2020 and is promoted to League C|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=20 November 2018|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=23 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200923101729/http://ffk-kosova.com/kosova-ben-histori-kalon-ne-play-off-per-euro-2020-dhe-promovohet-ne-ligen-c/|url-status=live}}</ref> After the successful Nations League, Kosovo entered the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]], where they shared [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying Group A|Group A]] with [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]], [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]], [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]] and 2018 World Cup fourth place finisher [[England national football team|England]].<ref name=UEFAEU2020Q-FFK>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/2018/12/02/kosova-ne-grup-me-angline/|title=Kosova në grup me Anglinë, ja deklaratat e presidentit Ademi dhe përzgjedhësit Challandes (Video)|trans-title=Kosovo in group with England, statements by president Ademi and selector Challandes (Video)|quote=Fati deshi që Kosova të tërhiqet në Grupin A, grup që përbëhet prej pesë ekipeve dhe aty krahas Kosovës do të jetë edhe gjiganti i futbollit, Anglia, pastaj Republika e Çekisë, Bullgaria dhe Mali i Zi|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=2 December 2018|access-date=23 December 2018|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090127/http://ffk-kosova.com/2018/12/02/kosova-ne-grup-me-angline/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Of eight matches, Kosovo won three times, against Bulgaria, Montenegro, and the Czech Republic, and finished third in the group. Their 5–3 loss to England on 10 September ended Kosovo's longest unbeaten run of 15 matches spanning nearly two years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/anglia-ndal-serine-e-shkelqyeshme-te-dardaneve/|title=Anglia ndal serinë e shkëlqyeshme të Dardanëve|trans-title=England stopped the excellent Dardanian series|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=10 September 2019|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=23 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200923040606/https://ffk-kosova.com/anglia-ndal-serine-e-shkelqyeshme-te-dardaneve/|url-status=live}}</ref> Although Kosovo failed go qualify directly, they advanced to the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying play-offs|qualifying play-offs]] because they had won their Nations League group.<ref name=Play-off>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/dardanet-pesojne-humbje-ne-ceki-endrra-per-kualifikim-vazhdon-tek-liga-e-kombeve/|title=Dardanët pësojnë humbje në Çeki, ëndrra për kualifikim vazhdon tek Liga e Kombeve|trans-title=Dardanians suffer losses in the Czechia, the dream of qualifying continues in the Nations League|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=14 November 2019|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=19 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200919154316/http://ffk-kosova.com/dardanet-pesojne-humbje-ne-ceki-endrra-per-kualifikim-vazhdon-tek-liga-e-kombeve/|url-status=live}}</ref> Kosovo was drawn against [[North Macedonia national football team|North Macedonia]] in the play-off semi-final, which they lost 1–2 in [[Skopje]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/endrra-euro2020-perfundon-dardanet-pesojne-humbje-ne-shkup/|title=Ëndrra "Euro2020" përfundon, Dardanët pësojnë humbje në Shkup|trans-title=The "Euro2020" dream ends, the Dardanians suffer losses in Skopje|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=8 October 2020|access-date=13 October 2020|archive-date=20 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020145342/http://ffk-kosova.com/endrra-euro2020-perfundon-dardanet-pesojne-humbje-ne-shkup/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In following competitions, Kosovo had less success. In the [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League]], Kosovo finished third with 5 points out of 6 matches, avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/kosova-ne-grup-me-greqine-sllovenine-e-moldavine/|title=Kosova në grup me Greqinë, Slloveninë e Moldavinë|trans-title=Kosovo in a group with Greece, Slovenia and Moldova|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=3 March 2020|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=1 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001040512/http://ffk-kosova.com/kosova-ne-grup-me-greqine-sllovenine-e-moldavine/|url-status=live}}</ref> In the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification|qualification campaign]] for the [[2022 FIFA World Cup]] in [[Qatar]], Kosovo finished last.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-i-jep-fund-serise-negative-e-mbyll-ciklin-kualifikues-me-barazim/|title=Kosova i jep fund serisë negative, e mbyll ciklin kualifikues me barazim|trans-title=Kosovo ends negative streak, closes qualifying cycle with draw|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=14 November 2021}}</ref> In the [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League]], Kosovo showed signs of improvement. The team finished second in [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|its League C group]], registering wins against [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-e-nis-me-fitore-edicionin-e-ri-ne-ligen-e-kombeve/|title=Kosova e nis me fitore edicionin e ri në Ligën e Kombeve|trans-title=Kosovo starts the new edition of the Nations League with a victory|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=2 June 2022}}</ref> [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-fiton-kunder-irlandes-se-veriut/|title=Kosova fiton kundër Irlandës së Veriut|trans-title=Kosovo wins against Northern Ireland|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=9 June 2022}}</ref> and losing to [[Greece national football team|Greece]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-shkelqen-ndaj-qipros/|title=Kosova shkëlqen ndaj Qipros|trans-title=Kosovo shines against Cyprus|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=27 September 2022}}</ref> During the UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying, Kosovo was placed in a group with [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]], [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]], [[Israel national football team|Israel]], [[Romania national football team|Romania]] and [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]]. Kosovo concluded the campaign in fourth place with 11 points out of 10 matches, missing out on qualification.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/dardanet-pesojne-humbje-nga-bjellorusia-ne-ndeshjen-e-fundit-kualifikuese-per-euro-2024/|title=Dardanët pësojnë humbje nga Bjellorusia në ndeshjen e fundit kualifikuese për Euro 2024|trans-title=Dardanians suffer defeat to Belarus in the last qualifying match for Euro 2024|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=21 November 2023}}</ref>

In the [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League]], Kosovo competed in [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|Group C2]], alongside [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]], [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]] and [[Romania national football team|Romania]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-serish-ne-grup-me-rumanine/|title=Kosova sërish në grup me Rumaninë|trans-title=Kosovo again in group with Romania|website=Football Federation of Kosovo|language=sq|date=8 February 2024}}</ref> The national team began the campaign with a home defeat to Romania but responded with several victories, including two wins over Cyprus and an away win against Lithuania.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-triumfon-ne-lituani-me-golat-e-zhegroves-e-krasniqit/|title= Kosova triumfon në Lituani me golat e Zhegrovës e Krasniqit|trans-title=Kosovo triumphs in Lithuania with goals from Zhegrova and Krasniqi|website=Football Federation of Kosovo|language=sq|date=12 October 2024}}</ref> The return match against Romania, held on 15 November 2024 in Bucharest, was abandoned and later awarded as a 3–0 forfeit loss to Kosovo.<ref name=UEFAroukos>{{cite web|url=https://disciplinary.uefa.com/0293-1c613c53754b-841e3c081bc3-1000/|title=UEFA Appeals Body decision announced|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=20 November 2024}}</ref> The decision came after tensions escalated during the match, involving clashes between players and fans, object-throwing, and physical altercations on the pitch,<ref name=BBCroukos>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c5yxl4qxp88t|title=Romania v Kosovo Nations League game abandoned after alleged Serbia chants|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=15 November 2024}}</ref> leading UEFA to end the game prematurely and issue disciplinary action.<ref name=FFKrou>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/vendimi-i-uefa-s-konfirmon-veprimet-e-arsyeshme-te-kombetares-se-kosoves-kunder-sjelljeve-raciste-dhe-mesazheve-provokuese-politike/|title=Vendimi i UEFA-s konfirmon veprimet e arsyeshme të Kombëtares së Kosovës kundër sjelljeve raciste dhe mesazheve provokuese politike!|trans-title=UEFA's decision confirms the reasonable actions of the Kosovo national team against racist behavior and provocative political messages!|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=20 November 2024}}</ref> Kosovo finished second in the group with 13 points, securing a spot in the [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League promotion/relegation play-offs#League B vs League C|promotion play-offs]] to League B.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-vazhdon-serine-pozitive-mposht-lituanine-ne-fadil-vokrri/|title=Kosova vazhdon serinë pozitive, mposht Lituaninë në 'Fadil Vokrri'|trans-title=Kosovo continues positive series, defeats Lithuania in 'Fadil Vokrri'|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=18 November 2024}}</ref>

====Promotion to League B and 2026 World Cup qualifying campaign====
In March 2025, Kosovo faced [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] in the promotion play-offs over two legs as part of the [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League promotion/relegation play-offs#League B vs League C|2024–25 UEFA Nations League play-offs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-ks.com/kosova-kunder-islandes-ne-play-off-per-ligen-b/|title=Kosova kundër Islandës në "play-off" për Ligën B|trans-title=Kosovo vs. Iceland in the "play-off" for League B|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=22 November 2024}}</ref> The first leg, played at home in [[Pristina]] on 20 March 2025, ended in a 2–1 victory for Kosovo.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosove-islande-foto/|title=Kosova triumfon në ndeshjen e parë të "play-off"-it për Ligën B|trans-title=Kosovo triumphs in the first play-off match for League B|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=20 March 2025}}</ref> Three days later, Kosovo secured a 3–1 win in the return leg on neutral ground in [[Murcia]], resulting in a 5–2 aggregate score and ensuring promotion to League B of the UEFA Nations League for the first time in its history.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-shkruan-histori-%E2%80%93-Dardan%C3%ABt-ngjiten-n%C3%AB-Lig%C3%ABn-B-t%C3%AB-Lig%C3%ABs-s%C3%AB-Kombeve-|title=Kosova shkruan histori – Dardanët ngjiten në Ligën B të Ligës së Kombeve|trans-title=Kosovo writes history – Dardanians ascend to League B of the Nations League|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=23 March 2025}}</ref>

In June 2025, Kosovo played two friendly matches at home, recording victories 5–2 against [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-triumfon-bindsh%C3%ABm-ndaj-Armenis%C3%AB-n%C3%AB-miq%C3%ABsore|title=Kosova triumfon bindshëm ndaj Armenisë në miqësore|trans-title=Kosovo triumphs convincingly over Armenia in friendly|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=6 June 2025}}</ref> and 4–2 against [[Comoros national football team|Comoros]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Rrahmani-me-%E2%80%9Chat-trick%E2%80%9D,-Kosova-vazhdon-serin%C3%AB-e-fitoreve|title=Rrahmani me "hat-trick", Kosova vazhdon serinë e fitoreve|trans-title=Rrahmani with a "hat-trick", Kosovo continues its winning streak|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=9 June 2025}}</ref> continuing their strong attacking form.

Kosovo then began their [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group B|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] campaign in September 2025. The team opened with a 0–4 away defeat to [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] in [[Basel]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-nis-me-humbje-kualifikimet-p%C3%ABr-Kup%C3%ABn-e-Bot%C3%ABs-2026|title=Kosova nis me humbje kualifikimet për Kupën e Botës 2026|trans-title=Kosovo starts 2026 World Cup qualifiers with loss|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=5 September 2025}}</ref> before responding with a 2–0 home win over [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]] in [[Pristina]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-shk%C3%ABlqen-n%C3%AB-%E2%80%9CFadil-Vokrri%E2%80%9D,-mposht-Suedin%C3%AB|title=Kosova shkëlqen në "Fadil Vokrri", mposht Suedinë|trans-title=Kosovo shines in "Fadil Vokrri", defeats Sweden|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=8 September 2025}}</ref> In October 2025, Kosovo drew goalless at home against [[Slovenia national football team|Slovenia]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-barazon-pa-gola-me-Sllovenin%C3%AB|title=Kosova barazon pa gola me Slloveninë|trans-title=Kosovo draws goalless with Slovenia|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=10 October 2025}}</ref> and followed it with a 1–0 away victory over Sweden in [[Gothenburg]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Fitore-madh%C3%ABshtore-e-Kosov%C3%ABs-n%C3%AB-Suedi|title=Fitore madhështore e Kosovës në Suedi|trans-title=Kosovo's magnificent victory in Sweden|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=13 October 2025}}</ref> The campaign concluded in November 2025 with two further positive results, as Kosovo defeated Slovenia 2–0 away in [[Ljubljana]], securing advancement to the play-offs,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Fitore-historike-n%C3%AB-Lubjan%C3%AB,-Kosova-siguron-%E2%80%9Cplay-off%E2%80%9D-in-e-Bot%C3%ABrorit-2026|title=Fitore historike në Lubjanë, Kosova siguron "play-off"-in e Botërorit 2026|trans-title=Historic victory in Ljubljana, Kosovo secures the 2026 World Cup play-offs|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=16 November 2025}}</ref> and drew 1–1 at home against Switzerland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-e-mbyll-me-barazim-me-Zvicr%C3%ABn,-tani-fokusi-n%C3%AB-%E2%80%98play-off%E2%80%99|title=Kosova e mbyll me barazim me Zvicrën, tani fokusi në 'play-off'|trans-title=Kosovo ends with a draw with Switzerland, now the focus is on the 'play-offs'|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=19 November 2025}}</ref>

On 26 March 2026, Kosovo defeated [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]] 4–3 in the semi-finals of the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA second round#Path C|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification play-offs]], advancing to their first-ever play-off final, in which they faced [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] in [[Pristina]], where they lost with a score of 1–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Kosova-ndalet-n%C3%AB-finale-t%C3%AB-%E2%80%9Cplay-off%E2%80%9D-it,-por-q%C3%ABndron-me-kok%C3%ABn-lart|title=Kosova ndalet në finale të "play-off"-it, por qëndron me kokën lart|trans-title=Kosovo stops in the play-off final, but stands with its head held high|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=31 March 2026}}</ref>

==Team image==
===Nicknames===
The Kosovo national football team has been known or nicknamed as the "''Dardanët''" ("[[Dardani]]ans"). In addition to the official nickname, the Kosovo national team had different nicknames in different periods as:
*"''Shqipëria B''" ("[[Albania national football team|Albania]] B") — During the period before 2016, the national teams of Kosovo and Albania have exchanged players with each other, which influenced these two teams to be nicknamed reserve (B) teams of each other, the Kosovo national team was nicknamed Albania B due to many players came to play for Kosovo as they had no space to play for Albania, but the same thing happened with the Albanian national team which was nicknamed Kosovo B due to of the large number of players of [[Kosovo Albanians|Kosovo Albanian]] descent in its composition.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kosova-a-kosova-b-2-2-ose-kosova-shqiperia-30-6/|title=Kosova A – "Kosova B" 2–2 ose Kosova – Shqipëria 30–6!|trans-title=Kosovo A – "Kosovo B" 2–2 or Kosovo – Albania 30–6!|language=Albanian|author=Besnik Dizdari|publisher=[[Panorama Sport]]|date=18 November 2015|access-date=13 July 2021|archive-date=2 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102165944/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kosova-a-kosova-b-2-2-ose-kosova-shqiperia-30-6/|url-status=live}}</ref>
*"''Ekipi i Kosovës''" ("Team Kosovo") — The Kosovo national team during the 2009–10 period was introduced with the nickname that was used as an alternative name in order to avoid possible sanctions by [[UEFA]] and [[FIFA]] against Kosovo and the opponents that Kosovo played. After Kosovo was allowed on 6 February 2013 by FIFA to play against FIFA member associations in international friendlies,<ref name=":0"/> this alternative name was no longer used.
*"''Brazili i Ballkanit''" ("[[Brazil national football team|Brazil]] of the [[Balkans]]") — The Kosovo national team was nicknamed the Brazil of the Balkans during their fifteen-match unbeaten run during the 2018–19 season.<ref name=FIFAplus/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2018/11/29/kosovo-brazil-balkans-consoled-nations-disappointment/ |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220112/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2018/11/29/kosovo-brazil-balkans-consoled-nations-disappointment/ |archive-date=12 January 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|title=How Kosovo, 'Brazil of the Balkans', consoled a nation's disappointment|work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]]|date=29 November 2018}}{{cbignore}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/12040/11805641/kosovo-the-brazil-of-the-balkans/|title=Kosovo: Who are England's next Euro 2020 opponents?|work=[[Sky Sports]]|date=10 September 2019|access-date=10 April 2021|archive-date=22 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210122170751/https://www.skysports.com/football/news/12040/11805641/kosovo-the-brazil-of-the-balkans|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Kits and crest===
{{Commons|Kosovo national football team kits}}
{{Football kit box
| pattern_la = _kosovo16h
| pattern_b  = _kosovo16h
| pattern_ra = _kosovo16h
| pattern_sh =
| pattern_so =
| leftarm    = 0000FF
| body       = 0000FF
| rightarm   = 0000FF
| shorts     = FFFF00
| socks      = 0000FF
| title      = Kosovo kit in the first competitive match against [[Finland national football team|Finland]] in September 2016.}}
The Kosovo national team kits were mostly red and black before the [[2008 Kosovo declaration of independence|declaration of independence]], with occasional variations over the years. After independence, the kits have primarily been blue and yellow. On 5 October 2016, Kosovo signed a four-year contract with the Spanish sportswear company [[Kelme (company)|Kelme]], becoming the first official kit supplier of Kosovo following the country's membership in UEFA and FIFA.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://telegrafi.com/nenshkruhet-marreveshja-mes-ffk-se-kelme-per-fanellat-e-perfaqesueses-se-kosoves-fotovideo/|title=Nënshkruhet marrëveshja mes FFK-së dhe 'Kelme' për fanellat e Përfaqësueses së Kosovës (Foto/Video)|trans-title=Agreement signed between FFK and Kelme for the Kosovo national team kits (Photo/Video)|language=sq|publisher=[[Telegrafi]]|date=5 October 2016|access-date=16 June 2018|archive-date=16 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180616130212/https://telegrafi.com/nenshkruhet-marreveshja-mes-ffk-se-kelme-per-fanellat-e-perfaqesueses-se-kosoves-fotovideo/|url-status=live}}</ref> On 23 February 2022, Kosovo signed a three-year contract with the Italian sportswear company [[Erreà]], making it the team's kit supplier.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/ffk-lidh-marreveshje-me-kompanine-e-veshjeve-sportive-errea/|title=FFK lidh marrëveshje me kompaninë e veshjeve sportive 'ERREA'|trans-title=FFK signs an agreement with the sportswear company 'ERREÀ'|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=23 February 2022|access-date=23 February 2023|archive-date=23 February 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230223145911/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/ffk-lidh-marreveshje-me-kompanine-e-veshjeve-sportive-errea/|url-status=live}}</ref> Starting from June 2026, the German sportswear company [[Adidas]] will become the team's next kit supplier.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://telegrafi.com/en/prezantohet-fanella-e-re-kosova-do-te-vishet-nga-marka-e-njohur-adidas/|title=New jersey presented, Kosovo to be outfitted by Adidas|publisher=[[Telegrafi]]|date=5 February 2026}}</ref>

===Home stadium===
{{multiple image|align=center|total_width=800
|image1   = Fadil Vokrri Stadium 2024.jpg
|caption1 = [[Fadil Vokrri Stadium]]
|image2   = Loro-Borici-Stadion.jpg
|caption2 = [[Loro Boriçi Stadium]]
|image3   = Olympisches Stadium "Adem Jashari" Mitrovicë.jpg
|caption3 = [[Adem Jashari Olympic Stadium]]
}}
Kosovo's home stadium is the [[Fadil Vokrri Stadium]]. The stadium capacity is 13,500, which makes it the second largest national [[List of football stadiums in Kosovo|stadium in Kosovo]]. Kosovo's previous national stadium was the [[Adem Jashari Olympic Stadium]] which is currently under renovation. Kosovo also used [[Loro Boriçi Stadium]] during the [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification&nbsp;– UEFA Group I#Matches|qualifying campaign]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rtklive.com/sq/news-single.php?ID=83148|title=Loro Boriçi, shtëpia e përkohshme e Kosovës|trans-title=Loro Boriçi, the temporary home of Kosovo|language=sq|publisher=[[Radio Television of Kosovo]]|date=28 June 2016|access-date=1 July 2016|archive-date=29 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160629202334/http://www.rtklive.com/sq/news-single.php?ID=83148|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/edirama.al/posts/10153918522541523|title=Loro Boriçi, i rilindur gati për kolaudim (Foto)|trans-title=Loro Boriçi, reborn ready for test (Photo)|language=sq|publisher=[[Edi Rama]]|website=[[Facebook]]|date=11 August 2016|access-date=11 August 2016|archive-date=5 April 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170405064844/https://www.facebook.com/edirama.al/posts/10153918522541523|url-status=live}}</ref> and this happened after the two stadiums in Pristina and Mitrovica at that time were under renovation and do not meet UEFA standards.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.telegrafi.com/keto-jane-11-stadiumet-e-superliges-dhe-asnjeri-nuk-ploteson-kushtet-foto/|title=Këto janë 11 stadiumet e Superligës dhe asnjëri nuk i plotëson kushtet (Foto)|trans-title=These are the 11 Superleague stadiums and none meet the conditions (Photo)|language=sq|publisher=[[Telegrafi]]|date=3 May 2016|access-date=1 July 2016|archive-date=25 May 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190525180252/https://telegrafi.com/keto-jane-11-stadiumet-e-superliges-dhe-asnjeri-nuk-ploteson-kushtet-foto/|url-status=live}}</ref> On 12 July 2019, the construction of [[Kosovo National Stadium]] began,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.botasot.info/futboll-sporti/1122218/fillon-ndertimi-i-stadiumit-kombetar-ne-kosove/|title=Fillon ndërtimi i stadiumit kombëtar në Kosovë|trans-title=The construction of the national stadium in Kosovo begins|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Bota Sot]]|date=12 July 2019|access-date=18 February 2021|archive-date=2 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102165832/https://www.botasot.info/futboll-sporti/1122218/fillon-ndertimi-i-stadiumit-kombetar-ne-kosove/|url-status=live}}</ref> and two days later the project of this stadium was presented which would have a capacity of 30,000 seats,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/u-prezantua-projekti-i-stadiumit-kombetar/|title=U prezantua projekti i Stadiumit Kombëtar|trans-title=The National Stadium project was presented|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=14 July 2019|access-date=18 February 2021|archive-date=20 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210620064134/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/u-prezantua-projekti-i-stadiumit-kombetar/|url-status=live}}</ref> but after the presentation, the construction was suspended for political reasons related to the location.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.koha.net/sport/248782/stadiumi-politik/|title=Stadiumi "politik"|trans-title="Political" Stadium|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Koha Ditore]]|date=6 December 2020}}</ref>
{{updated|31 March 2026|<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_venues.php?id=122|title=Kosovo team venues|quote=Only matches from 2014 are included|website=eu-football.info|access-date=31 March 2026}}</ref>}}

|}

===Rivalries===
====Albania====
{{main|Brotherly derby}}
This derby is otherwise known as Brotherly derby ({{langx|sq|Derbi vëllazëror}}), also known as the Brotherly ({{langx|sq|Vëllazërorja}}). The documented beginnings of this derby date back to the time during the [[World War II]], respectively on 29 November 1942, where they played a [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly match]] as part of the celebrations for 30th Anniversary of the Independence of Albania and the match ended with a 2–0 win for [[Albania national football team|Tirana]],{{efn|name=Tirana}}<ref name=DizdariI/><ref name=DizdariII/><ref name=Shehu/> this derby is back 50 years after the first match, when the [[Football Federation of Kosovo]] signed a cooperation protocol with the [[Albanian Football Association]] and in the framework of this protocol it was decided to play on 14 February a [[1993 Albania v Kosovo football match|friendly match]] between these two national teams,<ref name=Mevetesimi/> and this match ended with a 3–1 win for Albania.<ref name=RSSSFreport/>

====Romania====
Kosovo and [[Romania national football team|Romania]] have a tense football rivalry, largely due to Romania's support for Serbia's territorial integrity and [[International recognition of Kosovo|has not recognized the legitimacy]] of [[2008 Kosovo declaration of independence|Kosovo's independence]]. The two teams have faced each other several times, with each match resulting in significant controversy. In a September 2023 [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group I|UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying]], Kosovo walked off the pitch in protest after incidents involving Romanian fans, including pro-Serbia chants and banners.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://a2news.com/2023/09/12/baneri-kosova-eshte-serbi-nderpret-ndeshjen-ne-rumani|title=Baneri "Kosova është Serbi" ndërpret ndeshjen në Rumani|trans-title="Kosovo is Serbia" banner interrupts match in Romania|language=Albanian|publisher=A2 CNN|date=12 September 2023}}</ref> The match resumed, but Kosovo's appeal for a 3–0 victory was rejected and Romania won 2–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffk-kosova.com/kosova-nuk-reziston-me-10-lojtare-kunder-rumanise/|title=Kosova nuk reziston me 10 lojtarë kundër Rumanisë|trans-title=Kosovo cannot resist with 10 players against Romania|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=12 September 2023}}</ref> In their [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2024–25 UEFA Nations League]] encounters, tensions escalated further with fights on the pitch, object-throwing and clashes between players and fans.<ref name=BBCroukos/> The final match was abandoned and Romania was later awarded a 3–0 victory.<ref name=UEFAroukos/> Both teams faced fines for the incidents and the Football Federation of Kosovo subsequently confirmed they would appeal the decision to the [[Court of Arbitration for Sport]].<ref name=FFKrou/>

|rowspan="2"|2022–2024<ref>{{cite news|url=https://klankosova.tv/ndeshjet-e-kombetares-se-kosoves-ne-nations-league-nga-qershori-ne-klan-kosova-dhe-art-motion/|title=Ndeshjet e kombëtares së Kosovës në Nations League nga qershori në Klan Kosova dhe Art Motion|trans-title=Kosovo national team matches in the Nations League from June in Klan Kosova and Art Motion|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Klan Kosova]]|date=25 May 2022|access-date=25 May 2022|archive-date=6 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220606205734/https://klankosova.tv/ndeshjet-e-kombetares-se-kosoves-ne-nations-league-nga-qershori-ne-klan-kosova-dhe-art-motion/|url-status=live}}</ref>
|-
|[[Klan Kosova]]
|}
{{col-end}}

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Kosovo national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

|format     = 1
|date       = 10 October
|time       = 20:45
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group B|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|score      = 0–0
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044272/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|KOS}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|SVN}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Fadil Vokrri Stadium]]

==Coaching staff==
[[File:20180602 FIFA Friendly Match Austria vs. Germany Franco Foda 850 1423.jpg|thumb|[[Franco Foda]], the current head coach of Kosovo national football team.]]
===Current coaching staff===

|-
!Position
!Name
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Franco Foda]]
|-
|rowspan="3"|Assistant coach(es)
|{{flagicon|AUT}} [[Robert Ibertsberger]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Thomas Kristl]]
|-
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Atdhe Nuhiu]]
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|KOS}} Ahmet Beselica
|-
|Match analyst

|-
|Video analyst
|{{flagicon|AUT}} Robin Krasniqi
|-
|Team manager
|{{flagicon|KOS}} Bajram Shala

|Sporting director
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Samir Ujkani]]
|-
|Director of sport
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Muharrem Sahiti]]
|}

===Manager history===
[[File:Muharrem Sahiti (cropped2).jpg|thumb|[[Muharrem Sahiti]] has been the manager of the national team in three separate stints.]]
{{updated|7 June 2026|<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_managers.php?im=122|title=Kosovo national football team managers|quote=Only matches from 2014 are included|website=eu-football.info|access-date=18 November 2025}}</ref>}}
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!rowspan="2"|No.
!rowspan="2"|Name
!rowspan="2"|Period
!colspan="5"|Record
|-
!{{Tooltip|G|Games managed}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Games won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Games drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Games lost}}
!{{Tooltip|Win %|Winning percentage}}
|-
|align="center"|1
|{{flagicon|Kosova}} [[Ajet Shosholli]]
|align="center"|1993–2002
{{WDL|1|0|0|1}}
|-
|align="center"|2
|{{flagicon|KOS|UNMIK}} [[Bylbyl Sokoli]]
|align="center"|2002–2005
{{WDL|1|0|0|1}}
|-
|align="center"|3
|{{flagicon|KOS|UNMIK}} [[Muharrem Sahiti]]
|align="center"|2005–2006
{{WDL|3|2|0|1}}
|-
|align="center"|4
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Edmond Rugova]]
|align="center"|2006–2009
{{WDL|1|1|0|0}}
|-
|align="center"|5
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Albert Bunjaki]]
|align="center"|2009–2017
{{WDL|18|3|3|12}}
|-
|align="center"|{{Abbr|C|Caretaker manager}}
|{{flagicon|KOS}} [[Muharrem Sahiti]]
|align="center"|2017–2018
{{WDL|1|1|0|0}}
|-
|align="center"|6<hr />{{Abbr|C|Caretaker manager}}
|{{flagicon|SUI}} [[Bernard Challandes]]<hr />→ {{flagicon|KOS}} [[Muharrem Sahiti]]{{efn|On 11 November 2020, [[Bernard Challandes]] tested positive with [[Coronavirus disease 2019|COVID-19]] and [[Muharrem Sahiti]] was named as his replacement for the November 2020 matches,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/njoftim-nga-ffk-2/|title=Njoftim nga FFK|trans-title=Notice from FFK|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=11 November 2020|access-date=7 September 2021|archive-date=7 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210907213823/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/njoftim-nga-ffk-2/|url-status=live}}</ref> against [[Albania national football team|Albania]] (2–1 away defeat),<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=16911|title=Albania vs Kosovo, 11 November 2020|website=eu-football.info|access-date=11 November 2020}}</ref> [[Slovenia national football team|Slovenia]] (2–1 away defeat),<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=17065|title=Slovenia vs Kosovo, 15 November 2020|website=eu-football.info|access-date=15 November 2020}}</ref> and [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]] (1–0 home win).<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=17090|title=Kosovo vs Moldova, 18 November 2020|website=eu-football.info|access-date=18 November 2020}}</ref>}}
|align="center"|2018–2021<hr />2020
{{WDL|40|17|8|15}}
|-
|align="center"|{{Abbr|C|Caretaker manager}}
|{{flagicon|SVN}} [[Primož Gliha]]
|align="center"|2021–2022
{{WDL|2|0|1|1}}
|-
|align="center"|7
|{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Alain Giresse]]
|align="center"|2022–2023
{{WDL|14|4|6|4}}
|-
|align="center"|8
|{{flagicon|SVN}} [[Primož Gliha]]
|align="center"|2023
{{WDL|6|2|2|2}}
|-
|align="center"|9
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Franco Foda]]
|align="center"|2024–present
{{WDL|23|14|2|7}}
|-
!colspan="3"|Total
{{WDLtot|110|44|22|44}}
|}

==Players==
{{Main|List of Kosovo international footballers}}
===Current squad===
*The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly matches]] against the [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]] and [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]], on 31 May and 7 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Foda-publikon-list%C3%ABn-p%C3%ABr-ndeshjet-miq%C3%ABsore-ndaj-%C3%87ekis%C3%AB-dhe-Andorr%C3%ABs|title=Foda publikon listën për ndeshjet miqësore ndaj Çekisë dhe Andorrës|trans-title=Foda publishes the list for the friendly matches against Czechia and Andorra|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=25 May 2026}}</ref>
*''Caps and goals are correct {{as of|2026|6|7|lc=y}}, after the match against [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]].''<ref name=Last>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=19425|title=Kosovo vs Andorra, 7 June 2026|website=eu-football.info|access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048317--kosovo-vs-andorra/|title=Kosovo–Andorra|publisher=[[UEFA]]|access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref>

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up for the team within the last twelve months and are still available and eligible for selection.
<!--Sorted by position, date of birth, caps, goals and most recent call-up.-->

{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Edon Zhegrova]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|3|31|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=5|club=[[Juventus FC|Juventus]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>WD</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bersant Celina]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|9|9|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=2|club=[[AIK Fotboll|AIK]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>CLUB</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Meriton Korenica]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|12|15|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[CFR Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ismet Lushaku]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|9|22|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Västerås SK Fotboll|Västerås SK]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Florian Haxha]]|age={{Birth date and age|2002|4|6|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Motor Lublin]]|clubnat=POL|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bledian Krasniqi]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|6|17|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Zürich|Zürich]]|clubnat=SUI|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kreshnik Krasniqi]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|12|22|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Strømsgodset Toppfotball|Strømsgodset]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|CZE}}, 31 May 2026<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Valon Berisha]]|age={{Birth date and age|1993|2|7|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=4|club=[[FC Zürich|Zürich]]|clubnat=SUI|latest=v. {{fb|TUR}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Florent Muslija]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|7|6|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=3|club=[[SC Freiburg]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|TUR}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Donat Rrudhani]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|5|2|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=2|club=[[FC Sion|Sion]]|clubnat=SUI|latest=v. {{fb|SVN}}, 15 November 2025<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Behar Neziri]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|1|6|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC St. Gallen|St. Gallen]]|clubnat=SUI|latest=v. {{fb|SVN}}, 15 November 2025<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Lirjon Abdullahu|age={{Birth date and age|2008|4|6|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Stuttgarter Kickers]]|clubnat=GER|latest={{nowrap|v. {{fb|SUI}}, 5 September 2025<sup>PRE</sup>}}}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Rilind Nivokazi]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|1|26|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Sion|Sion]]|clubnat=SUI|latest=v. {{fb|AND}}, 7 June 2026<sup>WD</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Milot Rashica]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|6|28|df=y}}|caps=64|goals=12|club=[[Beşiktaş J.K.|Beşiktaş]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|TUR}}, 31 March 2026<sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Vedat Muriqi]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|4|24|df=y}}|caps=68|goals=[[List of international goals scored by Vedat Muriqi|32]]|club=[[Fenerbahçe S.K. (football)|Fenerbahçe]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|TUR}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Not part of the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to injury.
*<sup>CLUB</sup> = Not part of the current squad after the club has not allowed him to join.
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the current squad due to non-injury issue.
<!--*<sup>COACH</sup> = Not available in next match due to suspended by the coach.
*<sup>DOC</sup> = Not part of the current squad due to problems with his documentation.
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Not available in next match due to red card suspension.
*<sup>U21</sup> = Was called up from national [[Kosovo national under-21 football team|U21]] squad.-->
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Main|Kosovo national football team records and statistics}}

:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Kosovo.''<ref>{{cite web|last1=Mamrud|first1=Roberto|url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/kosovo-recintlp.html|title=Kosovo - Record International Players|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=5 June 2024|archive-date=15 July 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240715203512/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/kosovo-recintlp.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Captains===
{{updated|18 November 2025|<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=122&data=10|title=Kosovan footballers by caps as captain|website=EU-Football.info|access-date=13 October 2025}}</ref>}}
{{multiple image
| total_width       = 333
| image1            = Samir Ujkani.JPG
| caption1          = [[Samir Ujkani]] is the second-most capped player as captain in the history of Kosovo, with 30 appearances.
| image2            = Anel Raskaj.JPG
| caption2          = [[Anel Rashkaj]] is the first captain who led Kosovo in the [[2014 Kosovo v Haiti football match|first international match]] against [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]].

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
On 9 June 2016, the UEFA Emergency Panel decided that Kosovo would join [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]], [[Finland national football team|Finland]], [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]], [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] and [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]] in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group I|Group I]], and also decided that [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia and Herzegovina]] and [[Serbia national football team|Serbia]] should not play against Kosovo for security reasons.<ref name=2018FIFAWCQ-FIFA/><ref name=2018FIFAWCQ-FFK/> On 5 September 2016, Kosovo made its debut in [[FIFA World Cup qualification]] with a 1–1 away draw against Finland, with Kosovo's equalizing goal being scored by newcomer [[Valon Berisha]] from a penalty kick in the 60th minute.<ref name=FINvKVX/> On 2 September 2021, Kosovo achieved their first win in the FIFA World Cup qualifications a 1–0 away win against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/58436/|title=Kosova realizon fitoren historike në kualifikimet e Botërorit, e mposht Gjeorgjinë|trans-title=Kosovo achieves historic victory in World Cup qualifiers, defeats Georgia|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=2 September 2021|access-date=2 September 2021|archive-date=2 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210902182227/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/58436/|url-status=live}}</ref>
<onlyinclude>{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan="10" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|[[FIFA World Cup|<span style="color:#FFFFFF">FIFA World Cup</span>]] record
!rowspan="26" style="background:#244AA5"|
!colspan="7" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|<span style="color:#FFFFFF">Qualification</span>]] record
|-
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Year

|{{flagicon|URU}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|FRA|1794}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]

|colspan="7" {{N/A|''Part of {{fb|Kingdom of Yugoslavia|name=Kingdom of Yugoslavia}}''}}

|{{flagicon|BRA|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]] to {{flagicon|ITA}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]

|-
|{{flagicon|KOR|1997}}{{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]] to {{flagicon|GER}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|colspan="9" {{N/A|''Not a FIFA member, under {{flagdeco|United Nations}} [[United Nations Interim Administration Mission in Kosovo|UNMIK]]''}}

|-

|colspan="9" rowspan="2" {{Pending|''To be determined''}}
|-
|{{flagicon|KSA}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]

|}</onlyinclude>

===UEFA European Championship===
On 2 December 2018, in [[Dublin]], it was decided that Kosovo should be part in [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying Group A|Group A]] of the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]], together with [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]], [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]], [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]] and 2018 World Cup fourth place finisher [[England national football team|England]].<ref name=UEFAEU2020Q-FFK/> On 25 March 2019, Kosovo made their debut on [[UEFA European Championship qualifying]] with a 1–1 home draw against Bulgaria and the draw goal was scored by [[Arbër Zeneli]] on the 61st minute.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/2019/03/25/kosova-e-pafat-i-nis-me-barazim-kualifikimet-per-euro-2020/|title=Kosova e pafat, debuton me barazim në kualifikimet për Euro 2020|trans-title=Unlucky Kosovo debuts with the draw in qualifications for Euro 2020|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=25 March 2019|access-date=26 March 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327101130/http://ffk-kosova.com/2019/03/25/kosova-e-pafat-i-nis-me-barazim-kualifikimet-per-euro-2020/|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 11 June 2019, Kosovo won their first qualifying match for a major tournament by defeating Bulgaria 3–2.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffk-kosova.com/kosove-e-mrekullueshme-shijon-fitore-historike-ne-bullgari/|title=Amazing Kosovo enjoys historic win in Bulgaria|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=10 June 2019|access-date=21 September 2020|archive-date=22 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200922011658/https://ffk-kosova.com/kosove-e-mrekullueshme-shijon-fitore-historike-ne-bullgari/|url-status=live}}</ref>
<onlyinclude>{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!colspan="10" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|[[UEFA European Championship|<span style="color:#FFFFFF">UEFA European Championship</span>]] record
!rowspan="20" style="background:#244AA5"|
!colspan="7" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|[[UEFA European Championship qualifying|<span style="color:#FFFFFF">Qualification</span>]] record
|-

!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Round

|}</onlyinclude>

===UEFA Nations League===
{| class="wikitable floatright" style="text-align:center;font-size:85%;"
|-
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}" colspan="2"|'''Kosovo's Nations League record'''
|-
!style="background:#d0A650;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#244AA5|2px}}"|First match
|{{fb|AZE}} 0–0 {{fb-rt|KOS}}<br />([[Baku]], Azerbaijan; 7 September 2018)
|-
!style="background:#d0A650;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#244AA5|2px}}"|Biggest win
|{{fb|MLT}} 0–5 {{fb-rt|KOS}}<br />([[Ta' Qali]], Malta; 17 November 2018)
|-
!style="background:#d0A650;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#244AA5|2px}}"|Biggest defeat
|{{fb|KOS}} 0–3 {{fb-rt|ROU}}<br />([[Pristina]], Kosovo; 6 September 2024)
|-
!style="background:#d0A650;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#244AA5|2px}}"|Best result
|38th place in [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25]]
|-
!style="background:#d0A650;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#244AA5|2px}}"|Worst result
|44th place in [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League|2020–21]]
|}
On 24 January 2018, in [[Lausanne]], it was decided that Kosovo should be part of [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D|League D]] in [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D#Group 3|Group 3]] of the [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League]], together with [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]], [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]] and [[Malta national football team|Malta]].<ref name=201819UEFANL-UEFA/><ref name=201819UEFANL-TCH/> On 7 September 2018, Kosovo made their debut in the [[UEFA Nations League]] with a 0–0 away draw against Azerbaijan.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2018/09/07/kombetarja-a-kthehet-e-pamposhtur-barazon-me-azerbajxhanin/|title=Kombëtarja A kthehet e pamposhtur, barazon me Azerbajxhanin|trans-title=National A team returns unbeaten, draw with Azerbaijan|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=7 September 2018|access-date=8 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180908203731/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2018/09/07/kombetarja-a-kthehet-e-pamposhtur-barazon-me-azerbajxhanin/|archive-date=8 September 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 10 September 2018, Kosovo achieved their first win in the UEFA Nations League, which was also the team's first-ever competitive win, a 2–0 home win against the Faroe Islands.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2018/09/11/kosova-e-nderon-me-fitore-fadil-vokrrin/|title=Kosova e nderon me fitore Fadil Vokrrin, merr kryesimin e Grupit 3 në Ligën D|trans-title=Kosovo honors with victory Fadil Vokrri, takes the lead of Group 3 in League D|language=sq|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=10 September 2018|access-date=8 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181009013414/http://www.ffk-kosova.com/federata/2018/09/11/kosova-e-nderon-me-fitore-fadil-vokrrin/|archive-date=9 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> Kosovo finished the league unbeaten (with four wins and two draws) and will be promoted to the next tier (C league) for the next edition.<ref name=201819UEFANL-FFKfinal/>
<onlyinclude>{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!colspan="22" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|[[UEFA Nations League|<span style="color:#FFFFFF">UEFA Nations League</span>]] record
|-
!colspan="12" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|League phase
!rowspan="8" style="background:#244AA5"|
!colspan="9" style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Finals
|-
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Season

|}</onlyinclude>

===Non-FIFA Tournament===
Kosovo for first time after the [[Kosovo War]] participated in a [[KTFF 50th Anniversary Cup|tournament]] held to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the [[Cyprus Turkish Football Association]]. Kosovo lost against the [[Northern Cyprus national football team|host]] with result 1–0 and won against [[Sápmi football team|Sàpmi]] with result 4–1.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/ncyp-50ann-05.html|title=TR Northern Cyprus - 50 Years Anniversary Cup 2005|publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation|RSSSF]]|date=4 November 2005|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=27 November 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221127020507/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/ncyp-50ann-05.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
<onlyinclude>{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Year
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|Round
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!style="background:#244AA5;color:white;{{box-shadow border|a|#D0A650|2px}}"|{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}

|'''Runners-up'''

|[[KTFF 50th Anniversary Cup#Kosovo|Squad]]

|}

==Head-to-head record==

* [[Brotherly derby]]

==Honours==
;Awards
*[[Unofficial Football World Championships]]: 8 September 2025–31 March 2026<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theufwc.com/team/kvx|title=Kosovo|publisher=[[Unofficial Football World Champions]]|date=8 September 2025}}</ref>

;Decoration
*{{flag|Kosovo}}: [[Orders, decorations, and medals of Kosovo#Presidential Medal of Merits|Presidential Medal of Merits]] (2025)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ffk-kosova.com/lajmet/Presidentja-Vjosa-Osmani-dekoron-Komb%C3%ABtaren-me-Medaljen-Presidenciale-t%C3%AB-Meritave|title=Presidentja Vjosa Osmani dekoron Kombëtaren me Medaljen Presidenciale të Meritave|trans-title=President Vjosa Osmani decorates the National Team with the Presidential Medal of Merit|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Football Federation of Kosovo]]|date=1 April 2026}}</ref>
